2026-05-16.night_summary: 1-1 night, +0.30u. The PHI @ PIT under 8.5 was the easy money — Cristopher Sánchez threw a complete-game shutout, striking out a career-high 13 while Bubba Chandler walked four in three innings for Pittsburgh, and the 6-0 final never sniffed the number. The MIA @ TB under 7.5 is the one that stings, but not because the read was wrong — through nine innings it was a 2-2 ballgame, exactly what the thesis called for, with the under alive by 3.5 runs. Then Hunter Bigge and the Rays' bullpen imploded in the 10th, surrendering 8 runs on 6 hits including a Javier Sanoja double that plated three, turning a clean under into a 10-5 final. Variance got us, not a bad model — that game had no business going over. Ladder sits at 1 of 10 — we're on the board, climbing.

2026-05-17.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.03u. The PHI @ PIT under was the cleanest win of the card — Zack Wheeler blanked Pittsburgh and the Phillies won 6-0, never threatening 7.5; 1.5 units, no drama. The Mets ML came through the hard way: New York trailed by four before Tyrone Taylor tied it with a homer and Carson Benge walked it off in the 10th for a 7-6 win. The SD @ SEA under was a model miss — Kirby got torched for six runs in 5.2 innings, including two homers off Gavin Sheets, dropping to a 10.50 ERA in three career starts against San Diego; the red flag was flagged and underweighted, and we own that one. Ladder sits at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-18.night_summary: 0-2 night, -2.50u — a rough one, but the stories behind the losses are different. The BAL @ TB under (ladder pick) was a model miss, plain and simple: Rogers sent 10 batters to the plate in a five-run second inning, then Tampa Bay batted around again in a four-run sixth capped by Caminero's three-run homer, and the final was 16-6 — more than double the 7.5 line before the fifth inning was over. We got that one wrong; a 1.54 WHIP screamed big-inning risk and we discounted it. The SA @ OKC pick is a different story — Thunder -6.5 was alive and competitive through regulation, with OKC clawing back from a 10-point deficit and the lead changing hands twice in the final two minutes, before Wembanyama's logo-range three in the first OT and a pair of sealing dunks in double OT finished it 122-115. Variance got us there, not a bad read. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — we start climbing again tomorrow.
